     2‚Üíname: obsidian-cli
     3‚Üídescription: Use the official Obsidian CLI (1.12+) for vault operations. Prefer CLI for index-powered ops (search, backlinks, tags, tasks, properties, bases). Fall back to file tools when Obsidian is not running or for simple read/write.
     4‚Üí---
     5‚Üí
     6‚Üí# Obsidian CLI ‚Äî Agent Reference
     7‚Üí
     8‚Üí## When to Use CLI vs File Tools
     9‚Üí
    10‚Üí**Use CLI** when you need Obsidian's index or app features:
    11‚Üísearch, backlinks, links, tags, tasks, properties, bases, templates, outline, orphans, unresolved links
    12‚Üí
    13‚Üí**Use file tools** (Read/Write/Edit/Grep/Glob) for:
    14‚Üísimple file read/write, bulk text replacement, grep across files ‚Äî no app dependency
    15‚Üí
    16‚ÜíRule of thumb: if Obsidian's index adds value, use CLI. If it's plain text manipulation, use file tools.
    17‚Üí
    18‚Üí## Syntax Basics
    19‚Üí
    20‚Üí```
    21‚Üíobsidian <command> [param=value ...] [flag ...]
    22‚Üí```
    23‚Üí
    24‚Üí- **Vault targeting**: `vault="My Vault"` as first param, or run from inside vault dir
    25‚Üí- **File targeting**: `path=exact/path.md` (vault-relative) vs `file=name` (link-style resolution)
    26‚Üí- **Params**: `key=value` ‚Äî quote values with spaces: `name="My Note"`
    27‚Üí- **Flags**: boolean switches, no `=` ‚Äî e.g. `silent`, `overwrite`, `counts`, `total`
    28‚Üí- **Multiline**: `\n` for newline, `\t` for tab in content strings
    29‚Üí- **Structured output**: `format=json` (search, base:query), `format=tsv` (properties, tags)
    30‚Üí- **Clipboard**: append `--copy` to copy output
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí## Key Commands
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí### Read & Write
    35‚Üí```sh
    36‚Üíobsidian read path=<path>                                    # read file content
    37‚Üíobsidian append path=<path> content="<text>"                 # append to file
    38‚Üíobsidian prepend path=<path> content="<text>"                # prepend after frontmatter
    39‚Üíobsidian create name=<name> content="<text>" silent          # create file (safe ‚Äî won't overwrite)
    40‚Üíobsidian create name=<name> template=<template> overwrite silent  # create from template
    41‚Üíobsidian move path=<from> to=<to>                            # move/rename file
    42‚Üíobsidian delete path=<path>                                  # move to system trash (safe)
    43‚Üí```
    44‚ÜíGotchas:
    45‚Üí- `create` without `silent` opens the file in Obsidian's UI ‚Äî always add `silent` during agent operations.
    46‚Üí- `create` doesn't auto-create directories ‚Äî use `mkdir -p` via Bash first if the parent folder doesn't exist.
    47‚Üí- `create` with `template=` may place the file in the template's configured folder, ignoring `path=`. Verify the actual path with `search` or `files` after creation.
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí### Daily Notes
    50‚Üí```sh
    51‚Üíobsidian daily                          # open today's daily note
    52‚Üíobsidian daily:read                     # read daily note content
    53‚Üíobsidian daily:append content="<text>"  # append to daily note
    54‚Üíobsidian daily:prepend content="<text>" # prepend to daily note
    55‚Üí```
    56‚Üí
    57‚Üí### Properties (Frontmatter)
    58‚Üí```sh
    59‚Üíobsidian property:set name=<key> value=<val> path=<path>              # set property
    60‚Üíobsidian property:set name=<key> value=<val> type=<type> path=<path>  # set with explicit type
    61‚Üíobsidian property:read name=<key> path=<path>                         # read one property
    62‚Üíobsidian property:remove name=<key> path=<path>                       # remove property
    63‚Üíobsidian properties path=<path>                                       # list all properties
    64‚Üíobsidian properties path=<path> format=tsv                            # list as TSV (key\tvalue)
    65‚Üí```
    66‚Üí
    67‚Üí### Search
    68‚Üí```sh
    69‚Üíobsidian search query="<text>"                          # search vault (file paths)
    70‚Üíobsidian search query="<text>" path=<folder> limit=10   # scoped search
    71‚Üíobsidian search query="<text>" format=json              # JSON array of paths
    72‚Üíobsidian search query="<text>" format=json matches      # structured JSON with line numbers (preferred)
    73‚Üíobsidian search query="<text>" matches                  # text format with matching lines
    74‚Üí```
    75‚ÜíTip: `format=json matches` returns `[{"file":"path","matches":[{"line":N,"text":"..."}]}]` ‚Äî use this for programmatic search.
    76‚Üí
    77‚Üí### Tags & Tasks
    78‚Üí```sh
    79‚Üíobsidian tags all counts                            # list ALL vault tags with counts
    80‚Üíobsidian tags all counts sort=count                 # sorted by frequency
    81‚Üíobsidian tag name=<tag>                             # files with specific tag
    82‚Üíobsidian tasks all todo                              # vault-wide open tasks
    83‚Üíobsidian tasks all done                              # vault-wide completed tasks
    84‚Üíobsidian tasks daily                                 # tasks from daily note
    85‚Üíobsidian tasks path=<path>                           # tasks in file/folder
    86‚Üíobsidian task ref="<path>:<line>" toggle            # toggle task status
    87‚Üíobsidian task ref="<path>:<line>" done              # mark done
    88‚Üí```
    89‚ÜíNote: `tags` without `all` lists tags for the active/specified file only.
    90‚ÜíNote: `tasks` without a scope (`all`, `daily`, `path=`) defaults to the active file ‚Äî use `tasks all` for vault-wide results.
    91‚Üí
    92‚Üí### Links & Graph
    93‚Üí```sh
    94‚Üíobsidian backlinks path=<path>          # incoming links to file
    95‚Üíobsidian backlinks path=<path> counts   # with link counts
    96‚Üíobsidian links path=<path>              # outgoing links from file
    97‚Üíobsidian unresolved                     # broken/unresolved links
    98‚Üíobsidian orphans                        # files with no incoming links
    99‚Üíobsidian deadends                       # files with no outgoing links
   100‚Üí```
   101‚Üí
   102‚Üí### Structure
   103‚Üí```sh
   104‚Üíobsidian files folder=<path> ext=md     # list files in folder
   105‚Üíobsidian folders                        # list all folders
   106‚Üíobsidian outline path=<path>            # heading structure of file
   107‚Üí```
   108‚Üí
   109‚Üí### Bases
   110‚Üí```sh
   111‚Üíobsidian bases                                          # list .base files
   112‚Üíobsidian base:query path=<path> format=json             # query a base
   113‚Üíobsidian base:query path=<path> view=<name> format=json # query specific view
   114‚Üí```
   115‚Üí
   116‚Üí### Templates
   117‚Üí```sh
   118‚Üíobsidian templates                        # list available templates
   119‚Üíobsidian template:read name=<name>        # read template source
   120‚Üíobsidian template:read name=<name> resolve # render with variables filled
   121‚Üí```
   122‚Üí
   123‚Üí## Safety Rules
   124‚Üí
   125‚Üí**Never run without explicit user request:**
   126‚Üí- `eval` ‚Äî arbitrary code execution
   127‚Üí- `delete permanent` ‚Äî bypasses trash
   128‚Üí- `plugin:install` / `plugin:uninstall` ‚Äî modifies plugin state
   129‚Üí- `dev:cdp` ‚Äî Chrome DevTools protocol access
   130‚Üí
   131‚Üí**Prefer safe patterns:**
   132‚Üí- Use `append` / `prepend` over full file overwrite
   133‚Üí- `create` without `overwrite` flag is safe (won't replace existing files)
   134‚Üí- Confirm with user before bulk property changes, file moves, or deletes
   135‚Üí
   136‚Üí## Diagnostics
   137‚Üí```sh
   138‚Üíobsidian vault                   # confirm CLI connection ‚Äî returns vault name, path, file count
   139‚Üíobsidian version                 # show CLI version (e.g. "1.12.1 (installer 1.11.4)")
   140‚Üí```
   141‚Üí
   142‚Üí## Error Handling
   143‚Üí
   144‚Üí| Symptom | Likely cause | Action |
   145‚Üí|---------|-------------|--------|
   146‚Üí| `obsidian version` fails | CLI not installed or not on PATH | Fall back to file tools |
   147‚Üí| Command hangs or times out | Obsidian app not running | Start Obsidian or use file tools |
   148‚Üí| "Unknown command" | CLI version too old | Run `obsidian help` to check available commands |
   149‚Üí| Empty results from search/tags | Vault index not ready | Wait a moment, retry, or use Grep as fallback |
   150‚Üí
   151‚Üí**General fallback**: if CLI is unavailable, use Read/Write/Edit/Grep/Glob for file operations. The CLI requires the Obsidian desktop app to be running.
   152‚Üí

1‚Üí---
     2‚Üíname: docstring-numpy
     3‚Üídescription: Convert all Python docstrings to NumPy style with RST examples. Systematically processes files in a user-defined scope, enforcing consistent docstring format.
     4‚Üí---
     5‚Üí
     6‚Üí# NumPy Docstring Standardizer
     7‚Üí
     8‚ÜíConvert Python docstrings to NumPy style and ensure each has an RST example block.
     9‚Üí
    10‚Üí## When to Use
    11‚Üí
    12‚Üí- User asks to standardize, fix, or convert docstrings
    13‚Üí- User mentions "numpy style", "docstring format", or "RST examples"
    14‚Üí- User wants consistent documentation across Python files
    15‚Üí- Preparing code for Sphinx documentation generation
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üí## Instructions
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí### 1. Clarify Scope
    20‚Üí
    21‚ÜíAsk the user:
    22‚Üí- **Scope**: Which files or directories to process?
    23‚Üí- **Public only?**: Process all functions or just public API? (default: all)
    24‚Üí
    25‚ÜíIf unclear, ask before proceeding.
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí### 2. Discover Files
    28‚Üí
    29‚ÜíFind Python files in scope:
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí```bash
    32‚Üífind /path/to/scope -name "*.py" -type f
    33‚Üí```
    34‚Üí
    35‚ÜíReport file count and confirm before proceeding.
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí### 3. Process Each File
    38‚Üí
    39‚ÜíFor each Python file:
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí1. Read the file
    42‚Üí2. Identify all docstrings (module, class, method, function)
    43‚Üí3. Convert each to NumPy style
    44‚Üí4. Add RST example if missing
    45‚Üí5. Write changes back
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí### 4. NumPy Docstring Format
    48‚Üí
    49‚ÜíEvery docstring must follow this structure:
    50‚Üí
    51‚Üí```python
    52‚Üídef function_name(param1, param2):
    53‚Üí    """Short one-line summary.
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí    Extended description if needed. Can span multiple lines.
    56‚Üí
    57‚Üí    Parameters
    58‚Üí    ----------
    59‚Üí    param1 : type
    60‚Üí        Description of param1.
    61‚Üí    param2 : type, optional
    62‚Üí        Description of param2. Default is X.
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üí    Returns
    65‚Üí    -------
    66‚Üí    type
    67‚Üí        Description of return value.
    68‚Üí
    69‚Üí    Raises
    70‚Üí    ------
    71‚Üí    ExceptionType
    72‚Üí        When this exception is raised.
    73‚Üí
    74‚Üí    Examples
    75‚Üí    --------
    76‚Üí    Basic usage example:
    77‚Üí
    78‚Üí    .. code-block:: python
    79‚Üí
    80‚Üí        from module import function_name
    81‚Üí        result = function_name("value", 42)
    82‚Üí
    83‚Üí    """
    84‚Üí```
    85‚Üí
    86‚Üí### 5. Section Order
    87‚Üí
    88‚ÜíUse this exact order (include only relevant sections):
    89‚Üí
    90‚Üí1. **Summary** - Single line, imperative mood ("Return" not "Returns")
    91‚Üí2. **Extended Summary** - Optional, after blank line
    92‚Üí3. **Parameters** - Function/method inputs
    93‚Üí4. **Returns** / **Yields** - What is returned
    94‚Üí5. **Raises** - Exceptions that may be raised
    95‚Üí6. **See Also** - Related functions/classes
    96‚Üí7. **Notes** - Implementation notes (RST allowed)
    97‚Üí8. **References** - Citations
    98‚Üí9. **Examples** - **Required** - RST code-block format
    99‚Üí
   100‚Üí### 6. Example Block Requirements
   101‚Üí
   102‚ÜíEvery docstring **must** have an `Examples` section:
   103‚Üí
   104‚Üí```python
   105‚ÜíExamples
   106‚Üí--------
   107‚ÜíBasic usage:
   108‚Üí
   109‚Üí.. code-block:: python
   110‚Üí
   111‚Üí    from entalmat.bulk import BulkStructure
   112‚Üí    structure = BulkStructure.from_file("POSCAR")
   113‚Üí    volume = structure.get_volume()
   114‚Üí
   115‚Üí```
   116‚Üí
   117‚ÜíRules:
   118‚Üí- Use `.. code-block:: python` directive for code examples
   119‚Üí- Add descriptive text before each code block ("Basic usage:", "Create with custom settings:", etc.)
   120‚Üí- Indent code 4 spaces after the directive
   121‚Üí- No need to show output (unless demonstrating expected results)
   122‚Üí- Keep examples minimal but functional
   123‚Üí- Import what you use
   124‚Üí- Multiple examples can be shown with separate descriptive headers
   125‚Üí
   126‚Üí### 7. Common Conversions
   127‚Üí
   128‚Üí#### Google ‚Üí NumPy
   129‚Üí
   130‚Üí**Before (Google):**
   131‚Üí```python
   132‚Üídef func(x):
   133‚Üí    """Summary.
   134‚Üí
   135‚Üí    Args:
   136‚Üí        x (int): Description.
   137‚Üí
   138‚Üí    Returns:
   139‚Üí        str: Description.
   140‚Üí    """
   141‚Üí```
   142‚Üí
   143‚Üí**After (NumPy):**
   144‚Üí```python
   145‚Üídef func(x):
   146‚Üí    """Summary.
   147‚Üí
   148‚Üí    Parameters
   149‚Üí    ----------
   150‚Üí    x : int
   151‚Üí        Description.
   152‚Üí
   153‚Üí    Returns
   154‚Üí    -------
   155‚Üí    str
   156‚Üí        Description.
   157‚Üí
   158‚Üí    Examples
   159‚Üí    --------
   160‚Üí    Basic usage:
   161‚Üí
   162‚Üí    .. code-block:: python
   163‚Üí
   164‚Üí        result = func(5)
   165‚Üí
   166‚Üí    """
   167‚Üí```
   168‚Üí
   169‚Üí#### Sphinx ‚Üí NumPy
   170‚Üí
   171‚Üí**Before (Sphinx/reST):**
   172‚Üí```python
   173‚Üídef func(x):
   174‚Üí    """Summary.
   175‚Üí
   176‚Üí    :param x: Description.
   177‚Üí    :type x: int
   178‚Üí    :returns: Description.
   179‚Üí    :rtype: str
   180‚Üí    """
   181‚Üí```
   182‚Üí
   183‚Üí**After (NumPy):**
   184‚Üí```python
   185‚Üídef func(x):
   186‚Üí    """Summary.
   187‚Üí
   188‚Üí    Parameters
   189‚Üí    ----------
   190‚Üí    x : int
   191‚Üí        Description.
   192‚Üí
   193‚Üí    Returns
   194‚Üí    -------
   195‚Üí    str
   196‚Üí        Description.
   197‚Üí
   198‚Üí    Examples
   199‚Üí    --------
   200‚Üí    Basic usage:
   201‚Üí
   202‚Üí    .. code-block:: python
   203‚Üí
   204‚Üí        result = func(5)
   205‚Üí
   206‚Üí    """
   207‚Üí```
   208‚Üí
   209‚Üí### 8. Type Annotation Handling
   210‚Üí
   211‚ÜíIf type hints exist in signature, still include types in docstring for clarity:
   212‚Üí
   213‚Üí```python
   214‚Üídef func(x: int) -> str:
   215‚Üí    """Summary.
   216‚Üí
   217‚Üí    Parameters
   218‚Üí    ----------
   219‚Üí    x : int
   220‚Üí        Description.
   221‚Üí
   222‚Üí    Returns
   223‚Üí    -------
   224‚Üí    str
   225‚Üí        Description.
   226‚Üí
   227‚Üí    Examples
   228‚Üí    --------
   229‚Üí    Basic usage:
   230‚Üí
   231‚Üí    .. code-block:: python
   232‚Üí
   233‚Üí        result = func(5)
   234‚Üí
   235‚Üí    """
   236‚Üí```
   237‚Üí
   238‚Üí### 9. Class Docstrings
   239‚Üí
   240‚Üí```python
   241‚Üíclass MyClass:
   242‚Üí    """Short summary.
   243‚Üí
   244‚Üí    Extended description.
   245‚Üí
   246‚Üí    Parameters
   247‚Üí    ----------
   248‚Üí    param1 : type
   249‚Üí        Description.
   250‚Üí
   251‚Üí    Attributes
   252‚Üí    ----------
   253‚Üí    attr1 : type
   254‚Üí        Description of instance attribute.
   255‚Üí
   256‚Üí    Examples
   257‚Üí    --------
   258‚Üí    Create an instance:
   259‚Üí
   260‚Üí    .. code-block:: python
   261‚Üí
   262‚Üí        obj = MyClass("value")
   263‚Üí        print(obj.attr1)
   264‚Üí
   265‚Üí    """
   266‚Üí```
   267‚Üí
   268‚Üí### 10. Edge Cases
   269‚Üí
   270‚Üí- **Private functions** (`_func`): Include docstrings but examples optional
   271‚Üí- **Dunder methods** (`__init__`): Document parameters, examples optional
   272‚Üí- **Properties**: Treat like attributes, use `Returns` section
   273‚Üí- **Abstract methods**: Document interface, example can show subclass usage
   274‚Üí- **Generators**: Use `Yields` instead of `Returns`
   275‚Üí
   276‚Üí### 11. Verification
   277‚Üí
   278‚ÜíAfter processing, verify with:
   279‚Üí
   280‚Üí```bash
   281‚Üí# Check docstring coverage (if pydocstyle installed)
   282‚Üípydocstyle --convention=numpy /path/to/scope
   283‚Üí
   284‚Üí# Or check with ruff
   285‚Üíruff check --select=D /path/to/scope
   286‚Üí```
   287‚Üí
   288‚Üí### 12. Summary Report
   289‚Üí
   290‚ÜíProvide:
   291‚Üí- Files processed
   292‚Üí- Docstrings converted
   293‚Üí- Examples added
   294‚Üí- Any docstrings skipped and why
   295‚Üí

    80‚Üí        description=(
    81‚Üí            "Inspect failing GitHub PR checks, fetch GitHub Actions logs, and extract a "
    82‚Üí            "failure snippet."
    83‚Üí        ),
    84‚Üí        formatter_class=argparse.ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter,
    85‚Üí    )
    86‚Üí    parser.add_argument("--repo", default=".", help="Path inside the target Git repository.")
    87‚Üí    parser.add_argument(
    88‚Üí        "--pr", default=None, help="PR number or URL (defaults to current branch PR)."
    89‚Üí    )
    90‚Üí    parser.add_argument("--max-lines", type=int, default=DEFAULT_MAX_LINES)
    91‚Üí    parser.add_argument("--context", type=int, default=DEFAULT_CONTEXT_LINES)
    92‚Üí    parser.add_argument("--json", action="store_true", help="Emit JSON instead of text output.")
    93‚Üí    return parser.parse_args()
    94‚Üí
    95‚Üí
    96‚Üídef main() -> int:
    97‚Üí    args = parse_args()
    98‚Üí    repo_root = find_git_root(Path(args.repo))
    99‚Üí    if repo_root is None:
   100‚Üí        print("Error: not inside a Git repository.", file=sys.stderr)
   101‚Üí        return 1
   102‚Üí
   103‚Üí    if not ensure_gh_available(repo_root):
   104‚Üí        return 1
   105‚Üí
   106‚Üí    pr_value = resolve_pr(args.pr, repo_root)
   107‚Üí    if pr_value is None:
   108‚Üí        return 1
   109‚Üí
   110‚Üí    checks = fetch_checks(pr_value, repo_root)
   111‚Üí    if checks is None:
   112‚Üí        return 1
   113‚Üí
   114‚Üí    failing = [c for c in checks if is_failing(c)]
   115‚Üí    if not failing:
   116‚Üí        print(f"PR #{pr_value}: no failing checks detected.")
   117‚Üí        return 0
   118‚Üí
   119‚Üí    results = []
   120‚Üí    for check in failing:
   121‚Üí        results.append(
   122‚Üí            analyze_check(
   123‚Üí                check,
   124‚Üí                repo_root=repo_root,
   125‚Üí                max_lines=max(1, args.max_lines),
   126‚Üí                context=max(1, args.context),
   127‚Üí            )
   128‚Üí        )
   129‚Üí
   130‚Üí    if args.json:
   131‚Üí        print(json.dumps({"pr": pr_value, "results": results}, indent=2))
   132‚Üí    else:
   133‚Üí        render_results(pr_value, results)
   134‚Üí
   135‚Üí    return 1
   136‚Üí
   137‚Üí
   138‚Üídef find_git_root(start: Path) -> Path | None:
   139‚Üí    result = subprocess.run(
   140‚Üí        ["git", "rev-parse", "--show-toplevel"],
   141‚Üí        cwd=start,
   142‚Üí        text=True,
   143‚Üí        capture_output=True,
   144‚Üí    )
   145‚Üí    if result.returncode != 0:
   146‚Üí        return None
   147‚Üí    return Path(result.stdout.strip())
   148‚Üí
   149‚Üí
   150‚Üídef ensure_gh_available(repo_root: Path) -> bool:
   151‚Üí    if which("gh") is None:
   152‚Üí        print("Error: gh is not installed or not on PATH.", file=sys.stderr)
   153‚Üí        return False
   154‚Üí    result = run_gh_command(["auth", "status"], cwd=repo_root)
   155‚Üí    if result.returncode == 0:
   156‚Üí        return True
   157‚Üí    message = (result.stderr or result.stdout or "").strip()
   158‚Üí    print(message or "Error: gh not authenticated.", file=sys.stderr)
   159‚Üí    return False
   160‚Üí
   161‚Üí
   162‚Üídef resolve_pr(pr_value: str | None, repo_root: Path) -> str | None:
   163‚Üí    if pr_value:
   164‚Üí        return pr_value
   165‚Üí    result = run_gh_command(["pr", "view", "--json", "number"], cwd=repo_root)
   166‚Üí    if result.returncode != 0:
   167‚Üí        message = (result.stderr or result.stdout or "").strip()
   168‚Üí        print(message or "Error: unable to resolve PR.", file=sys.stderr)
   169‚Üí        return None
   170‚Üí    try:
   171‚Üí        data = json.loads(result.stdout or "{}")
   172‚Üí    except json.JSONDecodeError:
   173‚Üí        print("Error: unable to parse PR JSON.", file=sys.stderr)
   174‚Üí        return None
   175‚Üí    number = data.get("number")
   176‚Üí    if not number:
   177‚Üí        print("Error: no PR number found.", file=sys.stderr)
   178‚Üí        return None
   179‚Üí    return str(number)
   180‚Üí
   181‚Üí
   182‚Üídef fetch_checks(pr_value: str, repo_root: Path) -> list[dict[str, Any]] | None:
   183‚Üí    primary_fields = ["name", "state", "conclusion", "detailsUrl", "startedAt", "completedAt"]
   184‚Üí    result = run_gh_command(
   185‚Üí        ["pr", "checks", pr_value, "--json", ",".join(primary_fields)],
   186‚Üí        cwd=repo_root,
   187‚Üí    )
   188‚Üí    if result.returncode != 0:
   189‚Üí        message = "\n".join(filter(None, [result.stderr, result.stdout])).strip()
   190‚Üí        available_fields = parse_available_fields(message)
   191‚Üí        if available_fields:
   192‚Üí            fallback_fields = [
   193‚Üí                "name",
   194‚Üí                "state",
   195‚Üí                "bucket",
   196‚Üí                "link",
   197‚Üí                "startedAt",
   198‚Üí                "completedAt",
   199‚Üí                "workflow",
   200‚Üí            ]
   201‚Üí            selected_fields = [field for field in fallback_fields if field in available_fields]
   202‚Üí            if not selected_fields:
   203‚Üí                print("Error: no usable fields available for gh pr checks.", file=sys.stderr)
   204‚Üí                return None
   205‚Üí            result = run_gh_command(
   206‚Üí                ["pr", "checks", pr_value, "--json", ",".join(selected_fields)],
   207‚Üí                cwd=repo_root,
   208‚Üí            )
   209‚Üí            if result.returncode != 0:
   210‚Üí                message = (result.stderr or result.stdout or "").strip()
   211‚Üí                print(message or "Error: gh pr checks failed.", file=sys.stderr)
   212‚Üí                return None
   213‚Üí        else:
   214‚Üí            print(message or "Error: gh pr checks failed.", file=sys.stderr)
   215‚Üí            return None
   216‚Üí    try:
   217‚Üí        data = json.loads(result.stdout or "[]")
   218‚Üí    except json.JSONDecodeError:
   219‚Üí        print("Error: unable to parse checks JSON.", file=sys.stderr)
   220‚Üí        return None
   221‚Üí    if not isinstance(data, list):
   222‚Üí        print("Error: unexpected checks JSON shape.", file=sys.stderr)
   223‚Üí        return None
   224‚Üí    return data
   225‚Üí
   226‚Üí
   227‚Üídef is_failing(check: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
   228‚Üí    conclusion = normalize_field(check.get("conclusion"))
   229‚Üí    if conclusion in FAILURE_CONCLUSIONS:
   230‚Üí        return True
   231‚Üí    state = normalize_field(check.get("state") or check.get("status"))
   232‚Üí    if state in FAILURE_STATES:
   233‚Üí        return True
   234‚Üí    bucket = normalize_field(check.get("bucket"))
   235‚Üí    return bucket in FAILURE_BUCKETS
   236‚Üí
   237‚Üí
   238‚Üídef analyze_check(
   239‚Üí    check: dict[str, Any],
   240‚Üí    repo_root: Path,
   241‚Üí    max_lines: int,
   242‚Üí    context: int,
   243‚Üí) -> dict[str, Any]:
   244‚Üí    url = check.get("detailsUrl") or check.get("link") or ""
   245‚Üí    run_id = extract_run_id(url)
   246‚Üí    job_id = extract_job_id(url)
   247‚Üí    base: dict[str, Any] = {
   248‚Üí        "name": check.get("name", ""),
   249‚Üí        "detailsUrl": url,
   250‚Üí        "runId": run_id,
   251‚Üí        "jobId": job_id,
   252‚Üí    }
   253‚Üí
   254‚Üí    if run_id is None:
   255‚Üí        base["status"] = "external"
   256‚Üí        base["note"] = "No GitHub Actions run id detected in detailsUrl."
   257‚Üí        return base
   258‚Üí
   259‚Üí    metadata = fetch_run_metadata(run_id, repo_root)
   260‚Üí    log_text, log_error, log_status = fetch_check_log(
   261‚Üí        run_id=run_id,
   262‚Üí        job_id=job_id,
   263‚Üí        repo_root=repo_root,
   264‚Üí    )
   265‚Üí
   266‚Üí    if log_status == "pending":
   267‚Üí        base["status"] = "log_pending"
   268‚Üí        base["note"] = log_error or "Logs are not available yet."
   269‚Üí        if metadata:
   270‚Üí            base["run"] = metadata
   271‚Üí        return base
   272‚Üí
   273‚Üí    if log_error:
   274‚Üí        base["status"] = "log_unavailable"
   275‚Üí        base["error"] = log_error
   276‚Üí        if metadata:
   277‚Üí            base["run"] = metadata
   278‚Üí        return base
   279‚Üí
   280‚Üí    snippet = extract_failure_snippet(log_text, max_lines=max_lines, context=context)
   281‚Üí    base["status"] = "ok"
   282‚Üí    base["run"] = metadata or {}
   283‚Üí    base["logSnippet"] = snippet
   284‚Üí    base["logTail"] = tail_lines(log_text, max_lines)
   285‚Üí    return base
   286‚Üí
   287‚Üí
   288‚Üídef extract_run_id(url: str) -> str | None:
   289‚Üí    if not url:
   290‚Üí        return None
   291‚Üí    for pattern in (r"/actions/runs/(\d+)", r"/runs/(\d+)"):
   292‚Üí        match = re.search(pattern, url)
   293‚Üí        if match:
   294‚Üí            return match.group(1)
   295‚Üí    return None
   296‚Üí
   297‚Üí
   298‚Üídef extract_job_id(url: str) -> str | None:
   299‚Üí    if not url:
   300‚Üí        return None
   301‚Üí    match = re.search(r"/actions/runs/\d+/job/(\d+)", url)
   302‚Üí    if match:
   303‚Üí        return match.group(1)
   304‚Üí    match = re.search(r"/job/(\d+)", url)
   305‚Üí    if match:
   306‚Üí        return match.group(1)
   307‚Üí    return None
   308‚Üí
   309‚Üí
   310‚Üídef fetch_run_metadata(run_id: str, repo_root: Path) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
   311‚Üí    fields = [
   312‚Üí        "conclusion",
   313‚Üí        "status",
   314‚Üí        "workflowName",
   315‚Üí        "name",
   316‚Üí        "event",
   317‚Üí        "headBranch",
   318‚Üí        "headSha",
   319‚Üí        "url",
   320‚Üí    ]
   321‚Üí    result = run_gh_command(["run", "view", run_id, "--json", ",".join(fields)], cwd=repo_root)
   322‚Üí    if result.returncode != 0:
   323‚Üí        return None
   324‚Üí    try:
   325‚Üí        data = json.loads(result.stdout or "{}")
   326‚Üí    except json.JSONDecodeError:
   327‚Üí        return None
   328‚Üí    if not isinstance(data, dict):
   329‚Üí        return None
   330‚Üí    return data
   331‚Üí
   332‚Üí
   333‚Üídef fetch_check_log(
   334‚Üí    run_id: str,
   335‚Üí    job_id: str | None,
   336‚Üí    repo_root: Path,
   337‚Üí) -> tuple[str, str, str]:
   338‚Üí    log_text, log_error = fetch_run_log(run_id, repo_root)
   339‚Üí    if not log_error:
   340‚Üí        return log_text, "", "ok"
   341‚Üí
   342‚Üí    if is_log_pending_message(log_error) and job_id:
   343‚Üí        job_log, job_error = fetch_job_log(job_id, repo_root)
   344‚Üí        if job_log:
   345‚Üí            return job_log, "", "ok"
   346‚Üí        if job_error and is_log_pending_message(job_error):
   347‚Üí            return "", job_error, "pending"
   348‚Üí        if job_error:
   349‚Üí            return "", job_error, "error"
   350‚Üí        return "", log_error, "pending"
   351‚Üí
   352‚Üí    if is_log_pending_message(log_error):
   353‚Üí        return "", log_error, "pending"
   354‚Üí
   355‚Üí    return "", log_error, "error"
   356‚Üí
   357‚Üí
   358‚Üídef fetch_run_log(run_id: str, repo_root: Path) -> tuple[str, str]:
   359‚Üí    result = run_gh_command(["run", "view", run_id, "--log"], cwd=repo_root)
   360‚Üí    if result.returncode != 0:
   361‚Üí        error = (result.stderr or result.stdout or "").strip()
   362‚Üí        return "", error or "gh run view failed"
   363‚Üí    return result.stdout, ""
   364‚Üí
   365‚Üí
   366‚Üídef fetch_job_log(job_id: str, repo_root: Path) -> tuple[str, str]:
   367‚Üí    repo_slug = fetch_repo_slug(repo_root)
   368‚Üí    if not repo_slug:
   369‚Üí        return "", "Error: unable to resolve repository name for job logs."
   370‚Üí    endpoint = f"/repos/{repo_slug}/actions/jobs/{job_id}/logs"
   371‚Üí    returncode, stdout_bytes, stderr = run_gh_command_raw(["api", endpoint], cwd=repo_root)
   372‚Üí    if returncode != 0:
   373‚Üí        message = (stderr or stdout_bytes.decode(errors="replace")).strip()
   374‚Üí        return "", message or "gh api job logs failed"
   375‚Üí    if is_zip_payload(stdout_bytes):
   376‚Üí        return "", "Job logs returned a zip archive; unable to parse."
   377‚Üí    return stdout_bytes.decode(errors="replace"), ""
   378‚Üí
   379‚Üí
   380‚Üídef fetch_repo_slug(repo_root: Path) -> str | None:
   381‚Üí    result = run_gh_command(["repo", "view", "--json", "nameWithOwner"], cwd=repo_root)
   382‚Üí    if result.returncode != 0:
   383‚Üí        return None
   384‚Üí    try:
   385‚Üí        data = json.loads(result.stdout or "{}")
   386‚Üí    except json.JSONDecodeError:
   387‚Üí        return None
   388‚Üí    name_with_owner = data.get("nameWithOwner")
   389‚Üí    if not name_with_owner:
   390‚Üí        return None
   391‚Üí    return str(name_with_owner)
   392‚Üí
   393‚Üí
   394‚Üídef normalize_field(value: Any) -> str:
   395‚Üí    if value is None:
   396‚Üí        return ""
   397‚Üí    return str(value).strip().lower()
   398‚Üí
   399‚Üí
   400‚Üídef parse_available_fields(message: str) -> list[str]:
   401‚Üí    if "Available fields:" not in message:
   402‚Üí        return []
   403‚Üí    fields: list[str] = []
   404‚Üí    collecting = False
   405‚Üí    for line in message.splitlines():
   406‚Üí        if "Available fields:" in line:
   407‚Üí            collecting = True
   408‚Üí            continue
   409‚Üí        if not collecting:
   410‚Üí            continue
   411‚Üí        field = line.strip()
   412‚Üí        if not field:
   413‚Üí            continue
   414‚Üí        fields.append(field)
   415‚Üí    return fields
   416‚Üí
   417‚Üí
   418‚Üídef is_log_pending_message(message: str) -> bool:
   419‚Üí    lowered = message.lower()
   420‚Üí    return any(marker in lowered for marker in PENDING_LOG_MARKERS)
   421‚Üí
   422‚Üí
   423‚Üídef is_zip_payload(payload: bytes) -> bool:
   424‚Üí    return payload.startswith(b"PK")
   425‚Üí
   426‚Üí
   427‚Üídef extract_failure_snippet(log_text: str, max_lines: int, context: int) -> str:
   428‚Üí    lines = log_text.splitlines()
   429‚Üí    if not lines:
   430‚Üí        return ""
   431‚Üí
   432‚Üí    marker_index = find_failure_index(lines)
   433‚Üí    if marker_index is None:
   434‚Üí        return "\n".join(lines[-max_lines:])
   435‚Üí
   436‚Üí    start = max(0, marker_index - context)
   437‚Üí    end = min(len(lines), marker_index + context)
   438‚Üí    window = lines[start:end]
   439‚Üí    if len(window) > max_lines:
   440‚Üí        window = window[-max_lines:]
   441‚Üí    return "\n".join(window)
   442‚Üí
   443‚Üí
   444‚Üídef find_failure_index(lines: Sequence[str]) -> int | None:
   445‚Üí    for idx in range(len(lines) - 1, -1, -1):
   446‚Üí        lowered = lines[idx].lower()
   447‚Üí        if any(marker in lowered for marker in FAILURE_MARKERS):
   448‚Üí            return idx
   449‚Üí    return None
   450‚Üí
   451‚Üí
   452‚Üídef tail_lines(text: str, max_lines: int) -> str:
   453‚Üí    if max_lines <= 0:
   454‚Üí        return ""
   455‚Üí    lines = text.splitlines()
   456‚Üí    return "\n".join(lines[-max_lines:])
   457‚Üí
   458‚Üí
   459‚Üídef render_results(pr_number: str, results: Iterable[dict[str, Any]]) -> None:
   460‚Üí    results_list = list(results)
   461‚Üí    print(f"PR #{pr_number}: {len(results_list)} failing checks analyzed.")
   462‚Üí    for result in results_list:
   463‚Üí        print("-" * 60)
   464‚Üí        print(f"Check: {result.get('name', '')}")
   465‚Üí        if result.get("detailsUrl"):
   466‚Üí            print(f"Details: {result['detailsUrl']}")
   467‚Üí        run_id = result.get("runId")
   468‚Üí        if run_id:
   469‚Üí            print(f"Run ID: {run_id}")
   470‚Üí        job_id = result.get("jobId")
   471‚Üí        if job_id:
   472‚Üí            print(f"Job ID: {job_id}")
   473‚Üí        status = result.get("status", "unknown")
   474‚Üí        print(f"Status: {status}")
   475‚Üí
   476‚Üí        run_meta = result.get("run", {})
   477‚Üí        if run_meta:
   478‚Üí            branch = run_meta.get("headBranch", "")
   479‚Üí            sha = (run_meta.get("headSha") or "")[:12]
   480‚Üí            workflow = run_meta.get("workflowName") or run_meta.get("name") or ""
   481‚Üí            conclusion = run_meta.get("conclusion") or run_meta.get("status") or ""
   482‚Üí            print(f"Workflow: {workflow} ({conclusion})")
   483‚Üí            if branch or sha:
   484‚Üí                print(f"Branch/SHA: {branch} {sha}")
   485‚Üí            if run_meta.get("url"):
   486‚Üí                print(f"Run URL: {run_meta['url']}")
   487‚Üí
   488‚Üí        if result.get("note"):
   489‚Üí            print(f"Note: {result['note']}")
   490‚Üí
   491‚Üí        if result.get("error"):
   492‚Üí            print(f"Error fetching logs: {result['error']}")
   493‚Üí            continue
   494‚Üí
   495‚Üí        snippet = result.get("logSnippet") or ""
   496‚Üí        if snippet:
   497‚Üí            print("Failure snippet:")
   498‚Üí            print(indent_block(snippet, prefix="  "))
   499‚Üí        else:
   500‚Üí            print("No snippet available.")
   501‚Üí    print("-" * 60)
   502‚Üí
   503‚Üí
   504‚Üídef indent_block(text: str, prefix: str = "  ") -> str:
   505‚Üí    return "\n".join(f"{prefix}{line}" for line in text.splitlines())
   506‚Üí
   507‚Üí
   508‚Üíif __name__ == "__main__":
   509‚Üí    raise SystemExit(main())
   510‚Üí

Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me compile a comprehensive report of all the skills.

## Skills Exploration Report

I've thoroughly explored the `.cursor/skills/` directory (excluding playwright). Here's a comprehensive summary of each skill:

---

## **1. code-cleaner**
